interaction_phrase: published today in Nature
occurrence_sentence: Their results, published today in Nature, found no such decoherence down to extraordinarily small scales.

interaction_phrase: Credit: IceCube Collaboration
occurrence_sentence: Image: IceCube tests for fluctuations in the metric of spacetime that would break the normal rules of quantum mechanics for propagating neutrinos. The above graphic shows neutrinos interacting with virtual black holes fluctuating from the metric of spacetime, one possible mechanism of coherence loss. Credit: IceCube Collaboration

interaction_phrase: published today in Nature
occurrence_sentence: Their results, published today in Nature, found no such decoherence down to extraordinarily small scales.

interaction_phrase: co-led the analysis
occurrence_sentence: "Our results are over a million times stronger than the previous ones in well-motivated parts of the parameter space," Benjamin Jones, an associate professor at the University of Texas at Arlington (UTA), who co-led the analysis, said in a [story on the observatory's website](https://icecube.wisc.edu/news/research/2024/03/icecube-search-for-neutrino-decoherence-from-quantum-gravity/).

interaction_phrase: co-led by UTA PhD student Akshima Negi, assistant professor at the Niels Bohr Institute Tom Stuttard
occurrence_sentence: The analysis was also co-led by UTA PhD student Akshima Negi, assistant professor at the Niels Bohr Institute Tom Stuttard, and former UTA PhD student Grant Parker.

interaction_phrase: IceCube constraints
occurrence_sentence: "In the area of quantum gravitational decoherence, the IceCube constraints are now sufficiently strong that it is unlikely that any neutrino experiment will surpass their sensitivity in the near term," said Jones.
